commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5f597ef6410136db31f85bb4bb0b2ec5844ebd80)
commands.review_summary:
The PR reuses an already-appended WebChat agent media transcript supplement as the final `chat` broadcast payload and adds focused gateway regression coverage for agent-run TTS audio.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +153. Total +178 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main appends agent-run media to the transcript but has no final WebChat broadcast path for non-source agent media finals. I did not execute the tests because this review was explicitly read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer revision plus real WebChat proof; the missing proof is not automation-safe and the code blocker should be reviewed before merge.

target.title: fix(webchat): broadcast agent TTS media final payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #81468.
- Bridges WebChat-managed agent-run TTS/media supplements into the final `chat` broadcast payload, so the live WebChat path receives the same audio attachment that was already appended to the transcript.
- Leaves the non-agent final assembly branch alone; this targets the agent-run path called out in the review of #81470.
- Preserves the existing transcript dedupe behavior by reusing the already-appended assistant-media message for broadcast instead of appending a second assistant transcript entry.

## Verification
- `COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack corepack pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Agent-run auto-TTS/audio replies could be persisted as a WebChat assistant-media transcript supplement while the live final WebChat `chat` event remained text-only or omitted the audio attachment. The result was generated audio appearing in history/assistant-media state but not in the real-time final broadcast consumed by WebChat.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at head `2c36ee765f0c1956c5bcd628cc7045b410da5207`, rebased onto `origin/main` `23e9bc8c0b61f37851136dc5da2311935e348f5d`. Dependencies were installed from `pnpm-lock.yaml` using project pnpm `11.2.2` via Corepack.

The regression proof uses the real `chat.send` Gateway handler test harness with a local MP3 file, an agent-run dispatch, and the WebChat final broadcast path. The test now captures the final `chat` payload and asserts that it contains the audio attachment block while the transcript still receives exactly one assistant-media update.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack corepack pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Evidence after fix:
```txt
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  87 passed (87)
Duration  6.10s
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 11.67s
```

The updated regression checks the WebChat final payload shape for the auto-TTS agent-run case:
```txt
state: final
message.role: assistant
message.content[0]: { type: "text", text: "Audio reply" }
message.content[1]: { type: "attachment", attachment: { kind: "audio", label: "tts.mp3", mimeType: "audio/mpeg", isVoiceNote: true } }
assistant transcript updates: 1
assistant transcript idempotencyKey: idem-agent-tts:assistant-media
```

Observed result after fix: The agent-run auto-TTS media payload is broadcast as the final WebChat assistant message with an audio attachment block, and the transcript remains deduplicated to one assistant-media supplement entry.

What was not tested: I did not run a live WebChat browser session with a real Microsoft/Xiaomi TTS provider credential. The proof is local and Gateway-level; it exercises the final `chat` broadcast payload and local audio attachment projection, but not browser autoplay or a live external TTS provider.

## AI Assistance Disclosure
This PR was prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed the changed code path and verified the targeted tests above.
